chunks.text: Regret may be a possession that only humans possess.
Looking back on past choices and imagining " if only I had done that " is a notion that requires complex thinking.
In high school, I spoiled a relationship with a girlfriend in my class.
In a trivial quarrel, I hurt her pride.
If only I had been more polite back then.
If only I had been able to perceive her feelings.
Unfortunately, the young me did not have that capacity.
On graduation day, she came up to me nervously, gave a short wave, and left.
Even after decades, I sometimes think of her.
Through rumors from a neighbor, I learned that she had built a happy family.
Along with relief, I also felt a faint sadness.
Regret is at times an unpleasant feeling.
But to confront regret is also part of growth.
We cannot change the past, but we can learn from it.
If I could go back, I would confidently tell her a single " thank you.
" I am slowly forgiving my past self for not having said it.
